title: podman leaves out cni ip leases if interrupted while building image
body:
/kind bug

The dangling IP cannot be recycled even if the worker container is removed by `buildah rm XXX-working-container`, and even `podman system reset`. While it's quite easy to grow a new dangling IP like:

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create a Dockerfile with `echo -e 'FROM alpine:latest\nRUN sleep 30' > Dockerfile`
2. `podman build -t test`
3. Press Ctrl-C before the sleep command finishes.
4. `podman rm -f test-working-container` or `buildah rm test-working-container`
5. Might also run `podman system reset`
6. Check `/var/lib/cni/networks/podman`, suppose one have default podman network setting.

One will find a new IP lease sits here with contents of `buildah-buildah1234567890\neth0`, and no way to reuse this IP unless by a manual `rm 10.88.X.Y`

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.3.1
API Version:  4.3.1
Go Version:   go1.17.13
Built:        Tue Nov 22 20:00:00 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```
      libseccomp: 2.5.3
  os: linux

    path: /run/podman/podman.sock
  security:
    apparmorEnabled: true
    capabilities: CAP_AUDIT_WRITE,CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_MKNOD,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_NET_RAW,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT

    version: |-
      slirp4netns version 0.4.7
      commit: unknown
      libslirp: 4.3.1-git
  swapFree: 0

  uptime: 0s

  graphRootUsed: 0

    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
```


**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked [the Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)?**

No

comments.body:
This is a buildah issue and should be moved there, I have no permission to do so.
I am also not sure how buildah currently handles signals?

For SIGINT or SIGTERM we could catch it and tear down properly but on SIGKILL the build process is just gone and we have no way to release the ip.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

If this would turn out to be unfixable, how about writing it into document / troubleshooting guides? But I'm not sure if the mentioning is worthwhile as no one has ever filed similar issues. I do think it's a common scenario when debugging Dockerfile though.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@nalind @flouthoc Does buildah handle SIGINT/TERM? It would need to call `.Teardown()` on the network backend.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@Luap99 Sorry for the delayed reply, yes it does handle `SIGTERM` as far as i can recall, I'll do the requested changes, thanks for pointing in the direction.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
